phase1.explanation:
Object class methods — every class inherits these:

toString():
• Returns string representation of object
• Default: ClassName@hashcode
• Always override for meaningful output
• Called automatically when printing

equals():
• Compares objects for equality
• Default: compares references (==)
• Override to compare content
• Must follow contract: reflexive, symmetric, transitive

hashCode():
• Returns integer hash value
• Used in hash-based collections (HashMap, HashSet)
• Must be consistent with equals()
• If equals() true, hashCode() must be equal

Contract: If two objects are equal, they must have the same hash code.

hashCode algorithm: multiply by 31 (prime) to reduce collisions.

phase2.errorMessage:
false
Set size: 2

(No compiler error — but the LOGIC is wrong! Duplicates can appear in HashSet.)

phase3.prompt:
Create a Book class with:
• Fields: title (String), author (String)
• Constructor
• Override toString() — returns "Book[title='X', author='Y']"
• Override equals() — based on title AND author
• Override hashCode() — consistent with equals

In main, create two identical books and test equals() and toString().
dialogueHints: equals should check if title and author are equal
hashCode should use title.hashCode() and author.hashCode()
Use @Override annotation on all three methods
phase3.openingDialogue: Every object has a toString — give it a makeover! 📝
